advantages: O(1) peek and O(log n) push/pop — perfect for repeated 'next-most-important' queries.
Array layout means no pointers and excellent cache locality.
heapify builds a heap from n items in O(n), faster than n × push.
Enables elegant top-k, beam search, k-way merge and Dijkstra-style algorithms.
disadvantages: Not ordered overall — only the root is guaranteed to be the extremum.
No O(log n) search for an arbitrary key (must scan O(n)).
Removing an arbitrary element is O(n) without auxiliary structures.
Python's heapq is min-only; max-heap requires negation.
